ddls:
CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the location */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the location is situated */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the location is situated */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the location is situated */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographical latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographical longitude of the location */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the location */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the location */,
  "building_type" TEXT /* Type of building (e.g., residential, commercial, industrial) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device (e.g., solar panel, smart meter) */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location where the device is installed */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the device was installed */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance check */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the device (e.g., active, inactive, under maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "energy_readings" (
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each energy reading */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device that collected the reading */,
  "day" INTEGER /* Day of the week (0-6) when the reading was taken */,
  "time" INTEGER /* Time of day (0-23) when the reading was taken */,
  "sun_intensity" REAL /* Solar radiation intensity */,
  "sun_time" REAL /* Sun time (in hours) */,
  "elec_intensity" REAL /* Electricity intensity */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Ambient temperature at the time of the reading */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Ambient humidity at the time of the reading */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* Wind speed at the time of the reading */,
  "precipitation" REAL /* Precipitation at the time of the reading */,
  "cloud_cover" REAL /* Cloud cover percentage at the time of the reading */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reading_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_energy_readings_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "solar_panels" (
  "panel_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each solar panel */,
  "panel_name" TEXT /* Name of the solar panel */,
  "panel_type" TEXT /* Type of solar panel (e.g., monocrystalline, polycrystalline) */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location where the solar panel is installed */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the solar panel was installed */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance check */,
  "warranty_expiration_date" TEXT /* Warranty expiration date of the solar panel */,
  "efficiency" REAL /* Efficiency of the solar panel in converting sunlight to electricity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("panel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "smart_meters" (
  "meter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each smart meter */,
  "meter_name" TEXT /* Name of the smart meter */,
  "meter_type" TEXT /* Type of smart meter (e.g., single-phase, three-phase) */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location where the smart meter is installed */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the smart meter was installed */,
  "last_calibration_date" TEXT /* Date of the last calibration */,
  "warranty_expiration_date" TEXT /* Warranty expiration date of the smart meter */,
  "measurement_type" TEXT /* Type of measurement (e.g., kWh, kW) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("meter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather station */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location where the weather station is situated */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the weather station was installed */,
  "last_calibration_date" TEXT /* Date of the last calibration */,
  "warranty_expiration_date" TEXT /* Warranty expiration date of the weather station */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "energy_consumption" (
  "consumption_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each energy consumption record */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location where energy was consumed */,
  "day" INTEGER /* Day of the week (0-6) when energy was consumed */,
  "time" INTEGER /* Time of day (0-23) when energy was consumed */,
  "energy_consumed" REAL /* Amount of energy consumed (in kWh) */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the energy consumed (e.g., grid, solar, battery) */,
  "cost" REAL /* Cost of the energy consumed */,
  "carbon_footprint" REAL /* Carbon footprint of the energy consumed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("consumption_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_energy_consumption_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "solar_power_generation" (
  "generation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each solar power generation record */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location where solar power was generated */,
  "day" INTEGER /* Day of the week (0-6) when solar power was generated */,
  "time" INTEGER /* Time of day (0-23) when solar power was generated */,
  "power_generated" REAL /* Amount of solar power generated (in kWh) */,
  "panel_id" INTEGER /* ID of the solar panel that generated the power */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the generated power (e.g., solar panel, battery) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("generation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_solar_power_generation_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_solar_power_generation_panel_id FOREIGN KEY ("panel_id") REFERENCES solar_panels ("panel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the user (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the energy data */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the energy data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device accessed */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location where the alert was triggered */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., high energy consumption, low solar power generation) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message for the alert */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Whether the alert has been resolved (boolean) */,
  "resolution_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecasts" (
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location being forecasted */,
  "forecast_date" TEXT /* Date when the forecast was generated */,
  "forecast_type" TEXT /* Type of forecast (e.g., energy consumption, solar power generation) */,
  "forecast_value" REAL /* Forecasted value (in kWh) */,
  "confidence_level" REAL /* Confidence level of the forecast */,
  "forecast_method" TEXT /* Method used for the forecast (e.g., machine learning, statistical model) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecasts_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)
